_comment: Hyperparameter configuration per dataset type and model. See docs/config/hyperparams_rationale.md for mathematical justification.

type_b._description: Coloured MNIST numbers. N=10,008. Regression task: image -> sentence embedding vector.

lr_scheduler.rationale: Halve LR when val_loss plateaus for 5 epochs. Allows fine-grained convergence without manual tuning.

alexnet128.rationale: AlexNet128 has ~20M params vs ~1.2M for custom CNNs. Lower LR and stronger WD prevent overfitting on the small Type-B dataset.

rationale.optimizer: Adam combines adaptive per-parameter LR (RMSProp) with momentum (SGD). Well-suited for regression tasks with non-uniform gradient magnitudes across embedding dimensions.
rationale.lr_1e4: 1e-3 causes divergence in MSE regression with high-dim targets (dim=768). 1e-4 is empirically stable for visual regression with Adam. See rationale doc for convergence analysis.
rationale.weight_decay_1e4: L2 penalty on FC layers prevents overfitting given N_train=8006. Equivalent to a Gaussian prior over weights with sigma=1/sqrt(lambda)=100.
rationale.dropout_0_5: 50% dropout on FC head provides an ensemble regularisation effect. Not applied to conv layers (already regularised by BatchNorm).

type_a._description: Coloured geometric shapes with spatial relationships.
type_a.rationale: Slightly higher LR than Type-B as Type-A sentences have richer vocabulary (colours, shapes, spatial words), requiring faster initial learning of more diverse features.

type_c.rationale: Same settings as Type-B. Type-C images are structured and simple; standard hyperparameters suffice.
